content.problemStatement:
For a set of positive integers SSS, let's define gcd(S)\mathrm{gcd}(S)gcd(S) as the greatest integer which divides each element of SSS. If gcd(S)=1\mathrm{gcd}(S) = 1gcd(S)=1, the set SSS is called coprime. For example, the set {7,12,15}\{7, 12, 15\}{7,12,15} is coprime, but {6,12,15}\{6, 12, 15\}{6,12,15} is not coprime, since every element of this set is divisible by 333.

Your task is to find an integer sequence A0,A1,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0‚Äč,A1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1‚Äč such that:


for each valid iii, 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§1091 \le A_i \le 10^91‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§109
A0,A1,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0‚Äč,A1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1‚Äč are pairwise distinct
for each valid iii, the set {Ai,A(i+1)%N}\{A_i, A_{(i+1)\%N}\}{Ai‚Äč,A(i+1)%N‚Äč} is not coprime (%\%% denotes the modulo operator)
for each valid iii, the set {Ai,A(i+1)%N,A(i+2)%N}\{A_i, A_{(i+1)\%N}, A_{(i+2)\%N}\}{Ai‚Äč,A(i+1)%N‚Äč,A(i+2)%N‚Äč} is coprime
It is possible that there is no solution. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If there is no solution, print a single line containing the integer ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
Otherwise, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers A0,A1,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0‚Äč,A1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1‚Äč.
